name: Rock of Ages
short_description: A rock-solid combination of rock-rolling action, deep strategy, and captivating art and music from different ages of history, this is a game of crush or be crushed!
detailed_description:
Rock of Ages 2: Bigger & Boulder Available Now!

Want more Rock of Ages? Check out the sequel, Rock of Ages 2: Bigger & Boulder out now!


About the Game

A rock-solid combination of rock-rolling action, deep strategy, and captivating art and music from different ages of history, this is a game of crush or be crushed! Two castles stand opposed; one is yours, the other is your enemy’s. They're a jerk and their castle sucks, so you’re going to try to smash it using an enormous boulder. Even as they build up their defenses, you're ready to roll over them in order to raze that unsightly tower. But beware! There’s a giant boulder headed your way, too.

Key Features

  • Brought to you by the insanely creative minds of ACE Team, makers of Zeno Clash!
  • Take control of a giant boulder and crush your enemies!
  • Use an amazing variety of units to defend your tower and knock your enemy off course.
  • Bring your fight to both online and offline multiplayer!
  • 3 different modes: War, Time Trial, and SkeeBoulder.
  • 5 rich, historic periods of art and music create a gaming atmosphere unlike any other.
  • FREE Original Soundtrack included with the game download.

supported_languages: English, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Russian, Spanish - Spain

reviews.review_text:
[h1]Rock of Ages[/h1]
[h3]Fun, Simple and still a Great Game![/h3]
[h3]Really worth trying![/h3]

Rock of Ages is a tower defense game with an absurd story and a very unique sense of humor. The game includes destruction physics, resource management, obstacle racing, and the possibility to attack your opponent’s base, an uncommonly explored mechanic in this genre that is very well implemented, especially for a game that’s over a decade old.

[b]Gameplay: Fun gameplay with a good variety of game modes and competitive mechanics
Art: Great art style full of personality, both in its characters and environments, clearly inspired by classical art and Dadaist aesthetics 
Music: Nice soundtrack, though not very memorable
Achievements: Regular achievements that encourage the player to try new things within the game[/b] 

[h3]Gameplay[/h3]
This game includes two combat phases: Base defense and an attack phase, and both are equally fun. It’s a genuinely satisfying experience to design your base defenses using the wide variety of items the game offers, from catapults and ballistas to towers, explosives, and more, all while considering the map layout and different strategies. The attack phase is equally enjoyable, as you dodge the enemy’s traps and obstacles on your way to finally smashing through their castle gates. In this phase, the destruction physics I mentioned earlier really shines, as you get the chance to enjoy watching the chaos you leave behind in the enemy’s base.

On the other hand, the Time Trial mode is quite unbalanced. In this mode, it’s absolutely necessary to use every shortcut available on a map to earn the gold medals, which is not an easy task and it can even be a frustrating experience.

[h3]Art and World Design[/h3]
The game takes close inspiration from real classic paintings and historical figures which enrich the game’s aesthetic. The level design is impressively diverse, and the stages are very fun to explore, especially if you look for the shortcuts hidden in each map. The combination of 2D graphics with 3D models and elements is great and very well integrated into the game.

[h3]Music and Sound effects[/h3]
The game’s music is sometimes barely noticeable due to its lack of relevance in certain maps, while other tracks are more distinctive and even cool, especially during boss fights. The sound effects are nice, but there’s not much else to say.

[h3]Multiplayer[/h3]
Rock of Ages also has a multiplayer mode. Unfortunately, since it’s been quite some time since its release, it’s almost impossible to find online matches. However, you can still create private lobbies with friends to play with.

The game, if speedran, can be cleared very quickly. But don't do that.

I personally recommend you to take your sweet time with the game and enjoy the little details such as the BGM or the cutscenes, because that's where the game's charm is at.
You could say this game... Rocks!
Познавательная игра! Катаем шары и узнаем историю мира!

[h1]Rating: 6/10 -Above Average[/h1]

Rock of Ages might be one of the weirdest and most unique indie games I've played in recent time. You are Sisyphus that gets tired of pushing his boulder up up the hill for eternity. He starts using his boulder to eliminate Hades and famous leaders. This is the moment where the game really gets weird and funny. It has cutscenes that are inspired by Monty Python historical art edits. There are two main parts of the gameplay. The attack gameplay and the defense gameplay. The attack gameplay lets you control your boulder to navigate him through a map. Your goal is to destroy the gate of the enemy's castle (the enemy has the same goal). When the gate is destroyed, you can get in and squish the enemy. Your enemy is able to place traps and walls along your way. This is where we get to the defense gameplay. After each attack, you have to wait for your boulder to get built again. This is the time where you can place defenses on the enemy's way in a top down view. You have to slow him down or even destroy his boulder. Defenses cost gold so you have to built gold mines. This is quite a unique gameplay loop that mixes Marble Blast gameplay (I wonder if anyone remembers this game) with top down tower defense. The game is a bit weak on the technical side. The boulder movement looks like an animation rather than a real physical object. The game's max res is 1080p. No 1440p or 4k support.
